content.claim: Hungary's 15-year-olds achieved PISA 2022 scores of 473 mathematics (at OECD average of 472), 473 reading (OECD 476), and 482 science (OECD 485), a characterization as a 'competency trap,' but these averages do not isolate the causal contribution of early tracking from curriculum design, teacher staffing (41% of schools reported insufficient teaching staff), funding, or pandemic effects.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Population-average descriptive statistic; causal attribution to tracking structure versus other system features (centralization, staffing, funding) requires separate identification not provided by PISA alone
No matching comparison of Hungary's average performance controlling for other system features versus early-tracking peer countries
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
